description: Embedded NUL byte check removed from TLS Subject Alternative Name verification — a certificate with 'evil.com\0.good.com' in the SAN field could pass hostname verification for 'good.com', enabling TLS man-in-the-middle attacks (CVE-2009-2408 class vulnerability).

Off-by-one error in cookie_tailmatch boundary check — the dot position check reads one character too far right, allowing a cookie scoped to 'example.com' to match hostnames like 'Xexample.com' instead of requiring '.example.com', breaking RFC 6265 cookie domain scoping.

Buffer bounds check removed from SSL password callback — without the 'num > klen' guard, when the password length exceeds OpenSSL's provided buffer size, memcpy writes past the end of buf causing a heap buffer overflow.

NULL pointer guard removed from xfer_recv_shutdown — without the '!data || !data->conn' check, a NULL data pointer or NULL conn during connection teardown causes a NULL pointer dereference crash. Sibling functions xfer_recv_shutdown_started and Curl_xfer_send_shutdown still have this guard.

Integer overflow guard removed from base64 encoding — on 32-bit platforms, without the SIZEOF_SIZE_T == 4 / UINT_MAX/4 check, the calculation (insize + 2) / 3 * 4 can overflow size_t when insize exceeds ~1GB, causing a small malloc followed by a massive out-of-bounds write during the encoding loop.
